gloss.en: a curve that bends right round

words.example: The road went in a huge loop around the lake.

gloss.en: steady support for someone or something

words.example: Can I count on your loyalty?

gloss.en: machines as a group, especially large ones

words.example: The factory uses heavy machinery.

gloss.en: containing or using magic

words.example: The book is full of magical powers.

gloss.en: an official judge in the lowest courts

words.example: He had to appear before the magistrate.

gloss.en: acting like a magnet

words.example: Iron is a magnetic material.

gloss.en: the great size or importance of something

words.example: We did not realize the magnitude of the problem.

gloss.en: main land of a country, not its islands

words.example: We took a boat to the mainland.

gloss.en: accepted as normal by most people

words.example: These ideas are now part of mainstream culture.

gloss.en: keeping something in good condition by regular care

words.example: The school pays for the maintenance of the buildings.

gloss.en: authority given to a government by voters

words.example: The new leader has a clear mandate from the people.

gloss.en: required by law

words.example: Wearing a seat belt is mandatory.

gloss.en: to show a feeling or quality clearly

words.example: His fear began to manifest in his shaking hands.

gloss.en: to control someone, often in a dishonest way

words.example: She uses her charm to manipulate people.

gloss.en: controlling someone, often in a dishonest way

words.example: This ad is a cynical manipulation of the elderly.

gloss.en: a book or text before it is printed

words.example: She sent her manuscript to the publisher.

gloss.en: the third month of the year

words.example: She was born in March.

gloss.en: small and not important

words.example: There was only a marginal improvement in the weather.

gloss.en: connected with the sea and its life

words.example: The reef is full of marine life.

gloss.en: the world of buying and selling goods

words.example: Companies must survive in the marketplace.

gloss.en: a covering worn over the face

words.example: The robbers wore a mask to hide their faces.

gloss.en: the cruel killing of many people

words.example: The massacre of innocent civilians shocked the world.

gloss.en: connected with or involving mathematics

words.example: He solved a hard mathematical problem.

gloss.en: behaving in a sensible, adult way

words.example: Jane is very mature for her age.

gloss.en: to increase something as much as possible

words.example: The company wants to maximize its profits.

gloss.en: serious and important

words.example: They had a meaningful discussion about the future.

gloss.en: the time between two events

words.example: My new email is coming, but for the meantime use the old one.

gloss.en: connected with the Middle Ages

words.example: We visited a medieval castle.

gloss.en: calming the mind in silence by focusing it

words.example: She found peace through yoga and meditation.

gloss.en: the main tune in a piece of music

words.example: The song has a beautiful melody.

gloss.en: a short official note within an organization

words.example: The boss sent a memo to all staff.

gloss.en: an account someone writes about their own life

words.example: She published a memoir about her childhood.

gloss.en: a structure built to remember an event or person

words.example: They built a war memorial for the soldiers.

gloss.en: an experienced person who advises someone

words.example: She was a friend and mentor to many young actors.

gloss.en: a person who buys and sells goods

words.example: He works as a wine merchant.

gloss.en: kindness toward someone you could punish

words.example: The prisoner begged for mercy.

gloss.en: used to stress how small something is

words.example: It took her a mere 20 minutes to win.

gloss.en: only; simply

words.example: It is not merely a job, but a way of life.

gloss.en: to combine two or more things into one

words.example: The two banks plan to merge next year.

gloss.en: joining two businesses into one

words.example: If the merger goes through, many jobs will be lost.

gloss.en: the quality of deserving praise or reward

words.example: It is a work of outstanding artistic merit.

gloss.en: a set of methods used for an activity

words.example: They changed the methodology of language teaching.

gloss.en: the middle part of something

words.example: Such beauty was unexpected in the midst of the city.

gloss.en: the yearly movement of birds or animals

words.example: We watched the seasonal migration of whales.

gloss.en: willing to use force to achieve aims

words.example: The protest was led by militant groups.

gloss.en: trained non-professional soldiers acting as an army

words.example: The state called out the militia to keep order.

gloss.en: a building for grinding grain into flour

words.example: The old mill has been turned into apartments.

gloss.en: very small in size or amount

words.example: The work was done at minimal cost.

gloss.en: to reduce something to the lowest level

words.example: Good hygiene helps to minimize the risk of infection.

gloss.en: getting coal or minerals from the ground

words.example: The town's economy depends on coal mining.

gloss.en: a government department with one area of work

words.example: The Ministry of Defence issued a statement.

gloss.en: extremely small

words.example: There were minute amounts of chemicals in the water.

gloss.en: an amazing event believed caused by God

words.example: It was a miracle that everyone survived the crash.

gloss.en: great mental or physical pain

words.example: Fame brought her nothing but misery.

gloss.en: giving a wrong idea or impression

words.example: The advertisement gave misleading information.

gloss.en: a weapon sent through the air to explode

words.example: The army fired a nuclear missile.

gloss.en: a large, possibly violent crowd

words.example: An angry mob gathered outside the building.

gloss.en: the ability to move easily between places or jobs

words.example: The new train improved social mobility.

gloss.en: to organize people to work toward an aim

words.example: The unions mobilized thousands of workers in protest.

gloss.en: neither very great nor very small

words.example: The class is for students of moderate ability.

gloss.en: a change made to improve something

words.example: The system needs some modification to work better.

gloss.en: the force that keeps something developing

words.example: The campaign for his release gathers momentum each day.

gloss.en: a man living apart in a religious community

words.example: The monk lives quietly in the monastery.

gloss.en: complete control of a trade or service

words.example: The company had a monopoly on television broadcasting.

gloss.en: principles of right and wrong behaviour

words.example: They debated matters of public morality.

gloss.en: a reason for doing something

words.example: There seemed to be no motive for the murder.

gloss.en: a person driving a car

words.example: The accident was reported by a passing motorist.

gloss.en: connected with a town or city government

words.example: They voted in the municipal elections.

gloss.en: felt or done equally by two people

words.example: Their friendship is based on mutual respect.

gloss.en: that is to say; specifically

words.example: We focus on our target audience, namely young women.

gloss.en: happening in all parts of a country

words.example: They launched a nationwide campaign.

gloss.en: connected with a country's navy

words.example: The ships left from the naval base.

gloss.en: failure to give enough care or attention

words.example: The buildings are crumbling from years of neglect.

gloss.en: located near or next to a place

words.example: They moved to a neighbouring town.

gloss.en: a structure a bird makes for its eggs

words.example: The birds built a nest in the tree.

gloss.en: remaining after nothing more is taken away

words.example: The company made a net profit of £500.

gloss.en: a regular report sent to members of a group

words.example: Our sailing club produces a monthly newsletter.

gloss.en: a suitable role or way of life

words.example: He eventually found his niche in sports journalism.

gloss.en: showing fine qualities people admire

words.example: He was a noble and honest leader.

gloss.en: a downward head movement showing agreement

words.example: She gave a nod to show she agreed.

gloss.en: to formally suggest someone for a role or prize

words.example: They will nominate her for the presidency.

gloss.en: formally suggesting someone for a job or award

words.example: Membership of the club is by nomination only.

gloss.en: a person formally suggested for a job or prize

words.example: He was chosen as the party's presidential nominee.

gloss.en: not aiming to make a profit

words.example: She works for a non-profit organization.

gloss.en: in spite of this fact

words.example: The book is long but, nonetheless, very entertaining.

gloss.en: silly or untrue ideas or statements

words.example: Reports that he has resigned are nonsense.

gloss.en: twelve o'clock in the middle of the day

words.example: We should be there by noon.

gloss.en: deserving attention; important

words.example: The project was a notable success.

gloss.en: especially; in particular

words.example: The house had drawbacks, most notably its price.

gloss.en: to officially tell someone about something

words.example: Competition winners will be notified by post.

gloss.en: well known for being bad

words.example: He is a notorious criminal.

gloss.en: new, different and often slightly strange

words.example: The phone has a novel feature.

gloss.en: a place where young children are cared for

words.example: Her youngest child is at nursery now.

gloss.en: a reason for opposing something

words.example: I'd like to come too, if you have no objection.

gloss.en: to force someone to do something

words.example: The law obliges parents to send their children to school.

gloss.en: to fill the mind so nothing else fits

words.example: He seems to obsess over every small detail.

gloss.en: a fixed idea that fills the mind unreasonably

words.example: Her fear of flying is bordering on obsession.

gloss.en: happening sometimes but not often

words.example: He works for us on an occasional basis.

gloss.en: something that happens or exists

words.example: Power cuts are a common occurrence here.

gloss.en: how likely something is to happen

words.example: The odds are in our favour this time.

gloss.en: something produced for others to use or enjoy

words.example: This is the latest offering from the writer.

gloss.en: the child or young of a person or animal

words.example: Parents often worry about their teenage offspring.

gloss.en: connected with how a system or business works

words.example: The team faced many operational difficulties.

gloss.en: to choose a particular course of action

words.example: After graduating she opted for a career in music.

gloss.en: connected with sight or light and sight

words.example: The picture creates an optical illusion.

gloss.en: a feeling that good things will happen

words.example: There was a mood of cautious optimism.

gloss.en: spoken rather than written

words.example: We had a test of both oral and written French.

gloss.en: connected with how something is arranged

words.example: There were major organizational changes within the party.

gloss.en: a person's basic beliefs about a subject

words.example: He never discusses his political orientation.

gloss.en: to first appear in a place or situation

words.example: The disease is thought to originate in the tropics.

gloss.en: the sudden start of something unpleasant

words.example: The outbreak of war shocked everyone.

gloss.en: a short trip for pleasure or education

words.example: We went on a family outing to the beach.

gloss.en: a shop selling a company's goods

words.example: The business has many retail outlets in this state.

gloss.en: someone's attitude to life and the world

words.example: Travel broadens your outlook.

gloss.en: a strong feeling of shock and anger

words.example: The judge's remarks caused public outrage.

gloss.en: a person not accepted by a group

words.example: Here she felt she would always be an outsider.

gloss.en: to fail to see or notice something

words.example: He seems to have overlooked one important fact.

gloss.en: too; excessively

words.example: I'm not overly fond of pasta.

gloss.en: to watch and make sure work is done right

words.example: United Nations observers will oversee the elections.

gloss.en: to turn over so the top is at the bottom
